content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real backend-resolution defect: the default final-review Codex entries are not pinned when a workspace sets `default_backend = "codex"` with `default_model`, so the stronger reviewer/arbiter defaults can be silently replaced.

## Votes

- `fr-1-4fcad17c`: Accept
  `target_for_family` applies `base_backend.model` before non-explicit role defaults, and the compiled final-review reviewer[0] plus arbiter are configured as family-only Codex selections. The existing regression test already proves reviewer[0] resolves to `workspace-default-model` under `default_backend=codex` + `default_model`; the arbiter goes through the same precedence path, so it is affected too. That means the intended `codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh` final-review contract is not reliably enforced and can be silently weakened by a common workspace setting.
